event_text: The Privilegium Minus raises Austria to a duchy within the Holy Roman Empire.
The Babenberg rulers gain Styria, linking it closely with Austria.
Rudolf I defeats Ottokar II at the Battle on the Marchfeld, securing Habsburg control in Austria.
The Habsburgs are invested with Austria and Styria.
The University of Vienna is founded.
Albert II of Habsburg becomes King of the Romans, beginning long Habsburg influence in imperial elections.
Austria is raised to an archduchy.
Ferdinand I gains the crowns of Bohemia and Hungary after the Battle of Mohács.
The first Ottoman siege of Vienna fails.
The Thirty Years War begins in Habsburg Bohemia and devastates Central Europe.
The second Ottoman siege of Vienna is lifted.
Maria Theresa begins her reign over the Habsburg lands.
Austria fights Prussia in the Seven Years War.
Francis II proclaims the Austrian Empire.
Francis II dissolves the Holy Roman Empire.
The Congress of Vienna redraws Europe after the Napoleonic Wars.
Revolutions break out across the Austrian Empire.
Franz Joseph becomes Emperor of Austria.
Austria is defeated by Prussia at Königgrätz, ending Austrian leadership in German affairs.
The Austro-Hungarian Compromise creates the Dual Monarchy.
Crown Prince Rudolf dies at Mayerling.
Universal male suffrage is introduced for elections to the Austrian Reichsrat.
Archduke Franz Ferdinand is assassinated in Sarajevo.
The Habsburg monarchy collapses and the Republic of German-Austria is proclaimed.
The Treaty of Saint-Germain defines post-war Austria.
Austrian Chancellor Engelbert Dollfuss is assassinated during a Nazi coup attempt.
Nazi Germany annexes Austria in the Anschluss.
Austria is liberated and occupied by Allied powers after the Second World War.
The Austrian State Treaty restores Austrian sovereignty and Austria declares permanent neutrality.
Austria joins the European Union.
Austria adopts the euro as physical currency.
